name: Truffle Torte
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Amaretti biscuits crushed finely with a rolling pin
recipeInstructions:
() You can get this from cake decorating stores or from the chemist (drugstore). If you REALLY can't get it, you can substitute the thickest corn syrup you can find.
1. Sprinkle the crushed cookies evenly over the base of a 9-inch cake tin.
2. Break the chocolate into pieces and put them in a heat-proof bowl with the liquid glucose and rum.
3. Fit the bowl over a pan of barely simmering water, then leave it until the chocolate has melted and become quite smooth. Stir gently.
4. Turn off the heat and let the mixture cool for about five minutes or until it feels just warm.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the cream until slightly thickened.
6. Fold half of the cream into the chocolate mixture, then fold that back into the remaining cream.
7. Spoon the mixture into the prepared tin and tap gently to spread evenly.
8. Cover with cling film (plastic wrap) and chill overnight.
9. Just before serving, run a palette knife around the edge to loosen the torte, give it a good shake, and turn it out onto a serving plate.
10. Dust the surface with sifted cocoa powder and, if desired, mark the top into serving sections. Serve with chilled pouring cream and, if available, a couple of tablespoons of Amaretti liqueur for an added touch.
